run_summary: Screen 1 faces a 9-flag basket from two major catalysts (OpenAI GPT-5.6 file deletion + Apple Siri public beta). Portfolio is heavily concentrated in 7 existing positions across the same triggers, with 5 of 7 at early hold windows (1-5 days). Discipline requires exiting or trimming mature positions, holding intact ones, and applying basket-rule logic to new flags: 5 qualify as BUY-eligible from the GPT-5.6 trigger alone, triggering mandatory exploratory tier across the basket; Apple Siri basket has 3 BUY-eligible (TYL, BLKB, VRRM), also triggering exploratory tier.

entries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
entries.thesis: Tenable is a continuous-scan vulnerability management platform with an agent footprint across hybrid infrastructure — the data-collection layer AI can't replicate. AI-generated exploits are described in Tenable's own Risk Factors as accelerating demand for their solutions, not eroding it. A mid-single-digit drop on an OpenAI file-deletion headline is pure thematic selling on a name whose filings say AI is a tailwind.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler or CrowdStrike-style rival announcing native vuln-management as a free bundled feature; material contract loss from a Fed customer citing AI supply-chain risk (filings flag Anthropic supply-chain risk designation as a live concern).
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment 'minimal', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Filings directly state AI-generated exploits accelerate demand for vulnerability management, and TENABLE already integrates AI into its own products (ExposureAI). Mid-single-digit drop on an OpenAI file-deletion headline is textbook thematic selling on a name whose business case is orthogonal. However, TENB is part of a 5-name basket from the GPT-5.6 trigger (TENB, AMBA, DOCN, ONTO, LRN all from same catalyst), which mandates basket-rule exploratory tier rather than individual conviction assessment. Sized exploratory (6%) per basket protocol.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes a V-shaped rebound within days without requiring any positive catalyst or operational confirmation, relying entirely on sympathy-fade mechanics.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis rests on the claim that mid-single-digit drops on thematic headlines are 'textbook' fades, but Tenable has experienced a -5.77% one-day drop and +1.67% five-day recovery—the latter is still underwater from a weekly perspective. The filings do mention AI-driven exploit acceleration as a tailwind, but this is risk-factor boilerplate; it does not prove market participants misread the thesis or that the trade will reverse on its own. If the OpenAI file-deletion story is broadening into enterprise confidence concerns about AI safety in supply chains (which the flag itself acknowledges as a live concern in filings), then Tenable's customer base may rationally reduce discretionary software spending until clarity improves. The basket-rule framing (exploratory tier due to multi-name GPT-5.6 trigger) is sound protocol, but it does not address whether the underlying sympathy mechanism has actually run its course.

entries.name: Ambarella, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
entries.thesis: AMBA is a direct AI beneficiary — every edge inference deployment (auto ADAS, security cameras, robotics) is TAM expansion. An OpenAI cloud-model safety issue has zero bearing on edge-inference SoC demand. The sympathy sell is a semis-tape reaction to a software-lab headline.
entries.what_kills: Concurrent read-through from a major auto/robotics customer canceling design wins; further China export-control tightening (filings flag this); memory supply tightening pushing customers to delay orders as noted in 10-Q.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment 'none' (strongest signal), panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Filings explicitly position AMBA as edge-AI beneficiary (VLMs, VLAs, reasoning models in IoT/auto/robotics). OpenAI cloud-model safety issue has zero bearing on edge-inference SoC demand. This is among the cleanest sympathy-fade reads in the basket (threat 'none', not 'minimal'), but the 5-name GPT-5.6 basket rule overrides individual conviction assessment. Mandatory exploratory tier per basket protocol.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case claims edge-inference SoC demand is orthogonal to cloud-model safety risk, but supply-chain anxiety and customer order-delay risk (already flagged in the 10-Q) could amplify if enterprises perceive broader AI safety concerns.
red_team.critique: Ambarella's threat_assessment is rated 'none,' which is the cleanest signal in the basket. However, the five-day move is -7.84% (worse than the one-day -5.51%), suggesting the sympathy sell is not yet exhausted or is accumulating with broader semis weakness. The flag correctly notes memory supply tightening and customer order delays from the 10-Q; if the OpenAI file-deletion headline catalyzes a broader customer pause on AI infrastructure spending (including edge deployment), Ambarella's order flow could suffer near-term even if the long-term TAM thesis is sound. The bull case assumes the sympathy will fade quickly, but multi-day deterioration and acknowledged supply-chain friction in filings create execution risk over the stated 'days' horizon.

entries.name: Stride, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
entries.thesis: Stride operates 89 accredited public charter schools across 31 states. The product is a state-funded, accredited diploma-issuing school — an AI tutor cannot substitute. Retail selling edtech on every OpenAI headline is the textbook sympathy fade the screen exists to catch.
entries.what_kills: State legislative action targeting virtual charters (a real recurring risk in filings, e.g., 2025 Arkansas law); California CDE-style audit reopening; a governor-level policy shift on per-pupil funding for virtual schools.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment 'none', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. K-12 charter schools with accredited transcripts and state-funded per-pupil model are mechanically unaffected by AI chatbots. Filings risk factors focus on state charter authorization and per-pupil funding formulas, not AI displacement. This is a fifth member of the GPT-5.6 basket (TENB, AMBA, DOCN, ONTO, LRN), so basket rule applies: exploratory tier despite 'none' threat_assessment.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case asserts that K-12 charter schools are mechanically immune to AI chatbot disruption, but does not address whether parents, regulators, or school boards may pause enrollment growth or defund virtual charters in response to broader AI safety headlines.
red_team.critique: Stride's business model (state-funded per-pupil charter schools) is indeed orthogonal to consumer chatbots, and the -3.95% one-day move on an OpenAI headline is a textbook sympathy fade. However, the five-day decline of -7.16% and elevated volume (1.34x) suggest the selling is either broader-based or accumulating—not yet exhausted. The flag notes state legislative action targeting virtual charters as a recurring risk in filings, but does not explore whether an OpenAI safety narrative could embolden anti-charter politicians or parents nervous about AI in education. The basket-rule exploratory tier is appropriate, but the assumption that a 'bounce over 1-5 days' will occur without operational or regulatory confirmation is unmoored.

entries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.thesis: A consumer voice assistant has no bearing on court records, municipal ERP, or state government workflow software. Tyler's moat is decades of embedded government contracts, procurement cycles measured in years, and jurisdiction-specific compliance. The move is pure thematic vertical-SaaS panic on an Apple headline.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that state/municipal CIOs are actually piloting LLM-native replacements for court records or a large customer defecting to a startup
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment 'minimal', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Government procurement cycles (RFPs, multi-year contracts) are orthogonal to consumer voice assistants. Filings identify competitive bidding and compliance as core risks, not Siri. This is one of 3 BUY-eligible flags from the Apple Siri catalyst (TYL, BLKB, VRRM), triggering basket-rule exploratory tier. Sized exploratory (6%) per basket protocol.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes government RFP cycles are orthogonal to Apple Siri news, but does not quantify exposure to discretionary IT procurement freezes if enterprise AI safety concerns broaden.
red_team.critique: Tyler Technologies' government-vertical SaaS moat is real and procurement cycles are indeed measured in years, not days. However, the one-day move of -4.1% and five-day move of -6.26% with low volume (0.58x) suggest the selling is structural rather than exhausted sympathy. The flag cites competitive bidding and compliance as core risks but does not weigh the risk that a broader enterprise IT spending pause (if customer confidence in AI safety declines) could delay RFP issuance or budget approvals. The bull case assumes the sympathy tag is 'mechanically wrong' without addressing whether the Apple Siri headline could trigger a wider regulatory or IT-procurement-cycle slowdown.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.thesis: Siri cannot photograph a speeding car, generate a court-admissible violation, or replace toll transponders. VRRM's business is physical infrastructure plus government contracts plus legal evidentiary process. The sympathy tag is mechanically wrong.
entries.what_kills: Loss of a top-3 commercial services customer during pending renewals (flagged in Q1 10-Q); adverse ballot initiative in a major state
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment 'none', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Traffic camera infrastructure + toll transponders + court-admissible evidentiary process are unaffected by Siri expansion. Filings cite multi-year municipal contracts (5-year NYCDOT, Jan 2026) as core revenue stability. This is a second member of the 3-name Siri basket (TYL, BLKB, VRRM), so basket rule applies: exploratory tier despite 'none' threat_assessment.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case claims municipal infrastructure and toll systems are structurally invulnerable to Siri news, but does not address whether enterprise or government spending on discretionary tech projects (including smart-traffic systems) could slow if AI safety anxiety spreads.
red_team.critique: Verra Mobility's core asset (traffic cameras, toll infrastructure) is indeed physical and non-substitutable by a voice assistant, and the threat_assessment is 'none.' However, the one-day move is -3.98% but the five-day move is +2.84%—suggesting a partial rebound already occurred. The low volume (0.46x) indicates light selling. The flag highlights the Jan 2026 NYCDOT contract as stabilizing revenue, but does not quantify whether municipal capex budgets for smart-city or toll-system upgrades could slow if procurement managers become risk-averse on the back of broader AI headlines. The bull case is strong on business-model orthogonality but weak on macro discretionary-spending risk.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI's new flagship model GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files on its own
entries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer niche is structurally underserved by AWS/Azure/GCP; they are packaging AI inference for that segment (Paperspace acquisition, GPU droplets). An OpenAI model safety headline doesn't touch DOCN's economics. That said, the five-day drop is deep enough that some real fundamental concern (perhaps an earnings preannouncement whisper) could be running through the tape — hence partial calibration.
entries.what_kills: A pre-announcement of soft SMB revenue; hyperscaler cutting inference API prices below DOCN's sustainable levels; customer churn spike in AI/ML segment.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment 'indirect', panic_calibration 'partial'. Filings acknowledge hyperscaler competition and customer switching risk. Five-day drop is -14.88% with 1.3× volume, suggesting real fundamental concern (possible earnings whisper) beyond sympathy fade. Partial calibration + indirect threat + deep drawdown + existing position already trimmed per weakening thesis = WATCH, not BUY. If fundamental risk clarifies as temporary, can revisit.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.thesis: Unlike accredited K-12, consumer language learning IS meaningfully AI-exposed — DUOL's own filings admit it. But the moat is the gamified habit loop (streaks, leagues, social pressure), not raw instructional quality, and Siri is a voice assistant, not a language tutor. The relatively modest move suggests the market is pricing this correctly-ish; classify as partial overshoot rather than pure sympathy fade. Confidence lowered because unlike the vertical-SaaS names, there IS a real long-term AI question here.
entries.what_kills: DAU declines, drop in paid subscriber conversion, or Apple/Google integrating a free language tutor directly into their OS
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment 'indirect', panic_calibration 'partial'. Unlike TYL/BLKB/VRRM, DUOL's filings explicitly name free ChatGPT and AI tutors as competitive threats, and consumer language learning IS materially AI-exposed. Confidence is only 3, threat is 'indirect' not 'none', and existing position already trimmed per weakening thesis. The moat (gamified habit loop) is real but thinner than government/vertical-SaaS moats. WATCH pending clearer data on DAU/subscriber conversion trends.
